title: Persecutors of the Church
speaker: Dr. John Battle
source_files: 05-07-23 - 1 Thessalonians 2_13-20 - Persecutors of the Church - GUEST SPEAKER - Dr. John Battle.txt

outline.title: Strength to Endure Persecution
subpoints: Christians cannot expect to be "carried to the skies on flowery beds of ease"
God gives strength for the specific trial at the time it is needed
The Holy Spirit equips even the weakest Christian for the challenge before them

outline.title: The Present Threat: Anxiety About Future Persecution
subpoints: The daily threat is not immediate martyrdom but anxiety about what may come
Anxiety about future suffering interferes with present duty
God provides strength each day for the needs of that day

outline.title: Our Attitude Toward Persecutors
subpoints: Jesus commanded: pray for those who spitefully use and persecute you
Not hatred and bitterness but love and example before persecutors
We may defend life as part of obedience to God's commands
If the time of suffering comes without defense, we bear the attitude of Christ
The persecutor may be saved — Paul himself was once a fierce persecutor

summary:
Dr. John Battle preaches from 1 Thessalonians 2:13-20 on the persecution of the church, drawing out the encouragement and instruction the text provides for believers facing suffering. He emphasizes that the Christian life makes no promise of easy passage — God does not call most believers to a life free of hardship, and the old song's hope of being "carried to the skies on flowery beds of ease" is not the calling of most Christians. Instead, God sovereignly provides strength exactly when and as it is needed: not in advance, not in greater measure than required, but precisely calibrated for each trial. The Holy Spirit ministers 109 units of strength when 109 units are required.

Dr. Battle identifies the present, daily threat not as immediate martyrdom but as anxiety about potential future suffering. This anxiety, if allowed to grow, will interfere with faithful duty in the present. The remedy is to trust that God provides grace each day, resting in him rather than being consumed by anticipation of trials not yet arrived. For those already experiencing persecution, the scriptural command is clear: pray for persecutors, turn the other cheek, be an example before them — not out of passive indifference but with the love Christ himself showed toward those who put him to death.

The sermon closes with a pastoral prayer that believers would be serious in their faith, trusting that if the call to suffer arrives they would not apostatize or betray others, but would stand strong through Christ's strength. God is to be seen as working even through persecution for his own purposes and out of love for his people.
transcript:
## Strength to Endure Persecution

To be ready ourselves. We can't expect to, like that old song says, be carried to the skies on flowery beds of ease. We cannot expect that. That is not the calling of most Christians. Well, they God give us that strength, that resolve. And we know that he gives strength to endure these persecutions. I'm often wondered, what, you know, if somebody was standing with a sword in front of my eyeball and, you know, a torch or something, a hot iron, and, you know, what I say, yes, I'm a Christian, I believe in Christ, you know, you know, when you think about that, we don't really have that much strength, I think, all the time. I think God gives us the strength we need for the situation that we face. And you may have 58 units of strength, and this is calling for 100 in 9 units of strength. But I believe when that time comes, Jesus said, don't worry what you'll say. God, the Holy Spirit will tell you. And I think there is, in God's Holy Spirit's ministry in our hearts, the common practice of giving Christians that 109 units of strength they need for that challenge that's before them. But he does it at the time that it's necessary. He doesn't give us all that strength ahead of time, but he gives it when it's needed.

## The Present Threat: Anxiety

So when we daily live and if that threat is moving in the future, the threat we face is not that immediate threat there, but the threat we face is anxiety and thinking about that and letting that interfere with doing our duty today. God gives us the strength each day for what we need. And we can be thankful that we can trust and rest in him and the weakest, you know, weakest Christian can do mighty things through the Holy Spirit when the time comes. And we've seen that over and over. Oh, may God be glorified in us.

## Our Attitude Toward Persecutors

What about our attitude towards the persecutors? Well, Jesus said, pray for those that the spitefully use you and persecute you. Someone slap you on one cheek, turn the other cheek. We have an attitude, not of hatred and bitterness toward those who are persecuting us, but rather of love that we need to pray for them and be an example before them and as far as possible, of course we defend our lives and the lives of others and that's part of our obedience to God's commands, but if the time comes when we have no weather defense and we must suffer for Christ, then we are to have that attitude that Jesus had toward those who persecuted him. And we don't know that person may get saved. You think about Paul, Paul says I was extremely bitter and hateful against the Christians and yet here he is the great apostle. God can do that and use our testimony in that way.

## Closing Prayer

Let us pray. Our Father and heaven, we thank you for the privilege it's ours to live for Christ and even to suffer for Christ if you call us to do so. And we pray that we would all be serious in our faith so that we are trusting that if that should come, that we would not fall away, that we would not apostasize or betray others, but that we would be strong for Christ through his strength. We pray that you would help us also to recognize that all these persecutions that come upon the church are ultimately a part of your plan of purpose and come from your own hand out of love for us. So we pray lower that you would help us then to be good testimonies and witnesses. We do pray for those who are even now suffering for Christ around the world. We pray would give strength and consistency and blessing and encouragement to them as they stand for Christ. We pray in Jesus name. Amen.
